content: Just as the linguistic definition of reflection frames the term as both a physical mirroring of light and an active, intentional cognitive review, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to integrate two equally core functions: not only correcting prior reasoning errors, but also "mirroring" the problem context by reflecting back external human value assumptions and implicit constraints that were not explicitly stated in the original prompt. This dual functional model of reflexion, drawn from its linguistic roots, addresses a common gap where current LLM self-reflection only focuses on refining output logic rather than reflecting unstated contextual biases.
